title: Meridian EU Private Equity Co-Invest
content:
The Meridian EU Private Equity Co-Investment Fund represents an exclusive alternative investment opportunity designed for our most sophisticated private wealth clients seeking exposure to European mid-market buyout transactions. This product operates under the Alternative Investment Fund Managers Directive (AIFMD) Article 22 framework, ensuring comprehensive risk management and transparency standards for professional investors.

Fund Overview: The strategy focuses on co-investment opportunities alongside established European private equity sponsors, typically participating in transactions valued between €100 million and €750 million. Our investment committee targets companies demonstrating sustainable EBITDA margins above 12% and revenue growth potential exceeding 8% annually. The fund maintains sector diversification across technology, healthcare, industrial services, and consumer goods, with geographic concentration in Germany, France, and the Benelux regions.

Investment Structure: Minimum commitment stands at €2.5 million, with a target fund size of €150 million across 15-20 underlying investments. Management fees are structured at 1.25% annually on committed capital during the investment period, reducing to 1.0% on net invested capital thereafter. Carried interest is set at 20% above an 8% preferred return hurdle, aligning interests between our investment team and client capital.

Risk Considerations: In accordance with MiFID II suitability requirements, clients must demonstrate substantial investment experience and financial capacity to absorb potential total loss. Liquidity constraints are significant, with typical holding periods ranging 4-7 years and no secondary market availability. Currency exposure to non-Euro denominated investments may reach 25% of fund assets, creating additional volatility. Default risk on underlying portfolio companies remains elevated given leverage ratios typically between 4.5x-6.0x EBITDA at acquisition.

Performance Expectations: Historical European co-investment strategies have generated net IRRs between 12-18% over full investment cycles, though past performance provides no guarantee of future results. Target net returns approximate 14-16% IRR with money multiples of 2.2x-2.8x over the fund lifecycle. Performance reporting follows IPEV valuation guidelines with quarterly NAV updates and annual audited statements.

Regulatory Framework: The fund operates as a Luxembourg-domiciled alternative investment fund, compliant with AIFMD transparency and reporting obligations. All investments undergo comprehensive ESG screening per Article 8 SFDR classification, incorporating sustainability risk assessments and climate transition considerations. Professional indemnity insurance coverage extends to €50 million, providing additional investor protection.

Client Suitability: This investment exclusively targets high net worth individuals and family offices meeting MiFID II professional client categorization, with investable assets exceeding €10 million and demonstrated private equity allocation experience. Comprehensive suitability assessments evaluate risk tolerance, liquidity needs, and portfolio concentration limits before client onboarding.

Operational Infrastructure: Fund administration is handled through our Luxembourg service provider, with monthly reporting on capital calls, distributions, and portfolio developments. Investor relations maintain regular communication regarding new investment opportunities and portfolio company updates, ensuring transparency throughout the investment lifecycle.
